Cold, but Moderation Begins

Last Updated Wed March 18 at 10:57AM by Ray Russell

The weather has been so extreme lately, let's orient everyone to what is "average" for mid-March... Valley highs are in the low 50s with lows in the low 30s (mid 40s and upper 20s on mountaintops).

Temperatures today remain more than 10 degrees below average, but the afternoon is less cold. Temperatures return to seasonal levels Thursday and continue to rise through the weekend. Skies are variably cloudy through the week, but precipitation likely holds off until Monday.

The "Mix and Match" Special Offer continues on all the Snowman O'Mometer Pint Glass and Mug (or you can long for a 5 Golfball Day with that pint glass as well). Buy one, and get another drinkware item at 40% off.

Forecast Discussion

High pressure is centered to our northeast. We remain in a cold northwest flow for most of today. But the high continues to shift east and then southward. By Saturday, it is anchored in the Gulf.

Our flow turns southwesterly, and moderating temperatures are the story for the rest of the week and through the weekend. Warm fronts pass to our west, keeping skies variably cloudy. Today is cold. Wednesday is seasonal. Temperatures continue to climb to unseasonably pleasant levels this weekend.

Monday, a backdoor front moves through from the north, pushing a cool wedge southward east of the Appalachians. A few showers are possible Monday and into Tuesday. We feel the cooler air by Monday afternoon. Seasonably cool weather continues Tuesday with lots of clouds.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for March 16, 2026

Quiet weather returns for western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia after a hyper-active Monday around the region. Severe weather is followed by a sharp cold shot and mountain snow shower episode, then we moderate nicely for late week through the weekend.

Mar 16, 2026
Mid-March Madness

A strong cold front crosses the Southern Appalachians Monday morning and moves into the Foothills Monday afternoon.

Rain and thunderstorms accompany the frontal passage, and severe thunderstorms are ...

Mar 16, 2026
Severe Weather Season Around The Corner

As our March weather has become more changeable and volatile, it's a reminder that severe weather season is almost upon us.

Last week was Severe Weather Awareness Week in North Carolina; This week is Virginia's turn.

While western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia may have less overall severe weather than our surrounding neighbors, we still have our fair share, and it's a great time to review your severe weather emergency/preparedeness procedures ahead of time.
